channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: A British man who was killed when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street packed with holiday revelers was identified Saturday as the stepson of a former nanny to the Royal Family. Prince William expressed shock and sadness at the news of the death of Edward Pettifer of London.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: ATLANTA, Ga. (AP) — Jimmy Carter 's extended public farewell began Saturday in Georgia, with the 39th U.S. president’s flag-draped casket tracing his long arc from the Depression-era South and family farming business to the pinnacle of American political power and decades as a global humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JEFF AMY and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Drivers skid and crash as wintry mix grips central US before moving east
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — Road conditions were deteriorating Saturday in the central U.S. as a winter storm brought a mix of snow, ice and plunging temperatures, with forecasts calling for the dreaded combo to spread eastward in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A cartoonist has decided to quit her job at the Washington Post after an editor rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing before President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know as snow, freezing rain and bitter cold heads through much of the US
item.description: A major winter storm began Saturday in the central U.S. and was forecast to move east over the next several days, producing heavy snow, significant ice and frigid temperatures, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Indie filmmaker Jeff Baena, Aubrey Plaza's husband, found dead at Los Angeles home
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Writer-director Jeff Baena, whose darkly comedic independent films included “The Little Hours” and who was married to his frequent creative collaborator Aubrey Plaza, has died. He was 47.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, ABC 4

item.title: 39,000 John Deere mowers recalled
item.description: About 39,000 John Deere ZTrak™ Zero Turn Mowers with Kawasaki Engines have been recalled because the voltage regulator inside the mower's engine can fail while in use or while the product is being stored.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio governor signs bill to charge public for police video footage
item.description: Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) signed a new measure into law earlier this week that permits police departments to charge the public to release camera footage from its officers — including dash, body or surveillance video.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Hundreds of animals killed in Dallas shopping center fire
item.description: A fire that broke out at a shopping center in Dallas on Friday morning killed more than 500 animals, most of which were small birds, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austrian Chancellor Nehammer says he will resign after talks on forming a new government fail
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian Chancellor Karl Nehammer said Saturday he will resign in the coming days after talks on forming a new government failed a second time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Take these steps to protect yourself from winter weather dangers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Winter weather brings various hazards that people have to contend with to keep warm and safe. These dangers can include carbon monoxide poisoning, hypothermia and frozen pipes that can burst and make homes unlivable.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: New Orleans attacker had suspected bomb materials at home, reserved truck weeks ago, officials say
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The man who rammed a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year's revelers in New Orleans had suspected bomb-making materials at his home and reserved the vehicle used in the deadly attack more than six weeks earlier, law enforcement officials told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, JACK BROOK, STEPHEN SMITH and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filmmaker and husband to Aubrey Plaza, Jeff Baena, dies by suicide: report
item.description: Film writer and director Jeff Baena, who is also known for his marriage to Hollywood actress Aubrey Plaza, has died at the age of 47, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: German leader is more worried about Musk's backing of a far-right party than his insults
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz says he's staying “cool” against critical personal comments made by Elon Musk but finds it worrying that the U.S. billionaire makes the effort to get involved in a general election by endorsing the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New Orleans attacker’s apparent loyalty to Islamic State group highlights persistent threat of lone wolf terrorism
item.description: The deadly Jan. 1, 2025, attack in New Orleans serves as a reminder of the persistent threat to the U.S. from individuals inspired by extremist Islamist groups.
item.dc:creator: Sara Harmouch, American University, Fox 5

item.title: Florida mom makes teen son turn himself in for crime
item.description: A 15-year-old boy accused of armed attempted robbery was brought to Florida deputies to be turned in, by his own mother.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Paleontologists learn from rare 'Teen Rex' specimen
item.description: Details about the juvenile Tyrannosaurus rex fossil discovered by three boys in North Dakota are beginning to take shape, including its size, age and how its body worked.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's Kelce! Retired Eagles great Jason Kelce tries his hand as a late-night television show host
item.description: Jason Kelce threw a late-night bash — and he brought his wife, his parents, a fan with a Phillie Phanatic tattoo inked on his navel, a Philly band and an uncensored (at least on stage) take on sports for the first of five episodes of “They Call It Late Night with Jason Kelce” on ESPN.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans attacker had suspected bomb materials at home, reserved truck weeks ago, officials say
item.description: Law enforcement officials say the man who rammed a truck into a New Year's crowd had suspected bomb-making materials at his home and reserved the rental vehicle used in the attack weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Jim Mustian, Kevin Mcgill And Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Massachusetts man finds family who sent messages in a bottle
item.description: After finding a bottle filled with messages in the sand, Douglas Turnbull was on a mission to connect the letters to their owners.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Szabo, NewsNation

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star Zachery Ty Bryan jailed in Myrtle Beach
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan was arrested this week on a domestic violence charge in Myrtle Beach, online jail records show.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected as House speaker after dramatic floor vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Mike Johnson won reelection to the House speakership on a first ballot Friday, pushing past GOP hard-right holdouts and buoyed with a nod of support from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Cybertruck explosion marks second high-profile incident at Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: The Cybertruck fire and explosion was preceded by a 2021 bomb threat that was meant to serve as a message to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, Fox 5

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the New Year's Day attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Investigators now believe the driver who barreled through a crowd of New Year’s Day revelers in New Orleans acted alone and found he posted several videos on social media pledging his support for the Islamic State group, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: Law enforcement arrested Forrest Kendall Pemberton after he allegedly traveled to Plantation, Fla., in search of AIPAC’s office with several firearms, including an AR-15 rifle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4
